recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — phone filming RCA monitor. Same lab as V25/V26. Slightly different angle (cabinet/door visible at right). 1920x1080 at 30fps. HEAVIEST DEBRIS in series — thick layer of suture remnants along top edge of perforated board, indicating many prior attempts in this session. Dark suture visible. Good framing — task area fills frame. Stryker tower at upper right.

frame_analyses.description: Drain centered on pink foam. No instruments. HEAVY debris along top of board — heaviest in series. Same RCA lab.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Many prior attempts this session. Fatigue risk high.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments entering from upper right. Blue handle visible. Suture loop visible at drain. TIMING STARTS ~8s.
Both instruments at drain. Blue instrument from right, metallic from left. Needle driving through drain. Active placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement in progress. Good bimanual coordination visible.

frame_analyses.description: Suture placed. Dark suture visible arcing upward/left from drain. Both instruments at drain. Transitioning to first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement complete ~28s (~20s). Consistent with series average.

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping/tightening. Large suture loop arcing high above the field. Blue instrument at upper right. Tightening in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw at 45s — wrapping with high loop arc. Good throw technique.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw in progress. Blue instrument from right at drain. Suture wrapping visible at drain center. Knot building. Suture loop extending upward.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw complete ~50s (~22s). Second throw wrapping.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw wrapping. Both instruments at drain. Complex suture wrapping visible. Dark suture loops around drain center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw complete ~68s (~18s). Third throw started.

frame_analyses.description: Still working on third throw. Both instruments at drain with complex suture wrapping. Blue instrument from right. Dark suture loops visible. Extended third throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw taking longer — possible difficulty with wrapping or tightening. 22+ seconds.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting phase. Knot visible at drain with X/star pattern. Blue instrument from upper right. Instrument from upper left. Suture tails extending from knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw complete ~95s (~27s — longest throw). Cutting started ~95s. 25s into cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Still cutting. LONG suture tails extending upward in large arcs from knot. Blue instrument at right. Two distinct tail arcs visible. Significant length remaining.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 40s into cutting. Long tails suggest difficulty getting close to knot for clean cuts.

frame_analyses.description: Near/at completion. Instruments appear withdrawn. Knot at drain center with short-to-moderate tails. Drain slit appears mostly closed. No obvious gap. Drain not avulsed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion ~148s. Total task time from first instrument: ~140s.

frame_analyses.description: Final state. Instruments fully withdrawn. Compact knot at drain center with X-pattern and moderate tails. Drain slit mostly closed. Drain not avulsed. Clean final result despite heavy debris on board.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final verification frame. Tails moderate length — not as clean as V24/V26.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 28-50s (22s). High loop arc visible during tightening at 45s. Surgeon's knot assumed.
Third throw 68-95s (27s). LONGEST THROW — may have had difficulty with wrapping or tightening. Possible re-wrap.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Marks not independently visible. Placement area appears correct. Conservative estimate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 163s, drain slit appears mostly closed with compact knot. Moderate-length tails. No obvious gap visible. Drain not avulsed. Confidence low due to monitor recording and heavy debris obscuring detail.

technique_summary: V27 at approximately 140 seconds represents a REGRESSION from V26's record-breaking 94s, returning to the V21-V25 plateau range. The primary regressions are in the third throw (27s vs V26's 14s — nearly double) and cutting (53s vs V26's 26s — also nearly double). Placement at 18s and the first two throws (22s + 18s = 40s) were solid and consistent, but the third throw and cutting combined consumed 80s of the 140s total (57%). The heavy debris on the board confirms this is deep into a practice session — likely post-V26 fatigue. The pattern of V26→V27 (94→140) mirrors V24→V25 (117→150): breakthrough followed by regression to plateau. The consistent element is that cutting reverts to 50-67s after brief improvements, suggesting the fast cutting (26-40s) is not yet automatic. FLS score of ~454 is mid-range for recent performances.
improvement_suggestions: CUTTING REGRESSED AGAIN: 53s vs V26's 26s. Cutting speed improvements remain episodic, not consolidated.
THIRD THROW at 27s is the longest in recent videos — possible fatigue-related difficulty with wrap or tightening.
The V26→V27 pattern (94→140) matches V24→V25 (117→150): breakthrough → fatigue regression. This is normal.
Placement (18s) and first two throws (40s) remain CONSISTENTLY excellent — these skills are consolidated.
The gap between peak and plateau cutting: 26s (V26) vs 53s (V27). Closing this gap is the #1 priority.
Consider ending sessions AFTER a breakthrough attempt rather than pushing further — diminishing returns evident.
DEBRIS LEVEL suggests 4+ attempts in this session. If V26 was attempt 2-3 and V27 was attempt 4-5, the fatigue pattern is expected.
strengths: Placement at 18s — consistent with best recent performances
First two throws at 40s total — stable, efficient
Drain not avulsed — 27 consecutive videos without avulsion
Drain closure appears good despite fatigue
Even the 'regressed' performance (140s) would have been a series best before V12

phase_comparison.throws_67s: REGRESSED. V26: 50s. V25: 58s. V24: 56s. Third throw (27s) is the outlier.
phase_comparison.cutting_53s: REGRESSED. V26: 26s. V25: 67s. V24: 40s. Back to mid-range.

key_observations: 140s / 454 FLS — regression from V26's 94s/500 back to V22-V25 plateau.
V26→V27 mirrors V24→V25: breakthrough → fatigue regression. Consistent pattern.
CUTTING: 53s vs V26's 26s — cutting improvements remain episodic, not consolidated.
THIRD THROW: 27s (longest in recent series) — possible fatigue or suture handling difficulty.
Placement + first 2 throws = 58s — only 8s slower than V26's 50s. Non-throw phases stable.
HEAVIEST DEBRIS in series — deep into practice session. Fatigue expected.
27th video. The pattern is clear: peaks (V14: 107, V24: 117, V26: 94) followed by plateau regressions.
The plateau itself has improved: early plateau ~150s (V22-V25), current ~140s (V27). Gradual upward trend.
Peak performance is clearly sub-100s capable. The gap between peak and plateau is closing.

series_statistics.cutting_variance: VERY HIGH — 26s to 67s. Most volatile phase.
